First.topic: Introduction to Speech and Language Skills
subtopics: Organs of speech and how sounds are produced
Introduction to speech work: pure vowel sounds (monophthongs)
The four language skills: Listening, Speaking, Reading, Writing
Vocabulary development: the family; school subjects
Reading strategy: SPQ3R (Survey, Preview, Question, Read, Recall, Review)
learning_objectives: Identify and name the organs of speech
Distinguish the four language skills and explain their importance
Apply the SPQ3R reading strategy to a passage
First.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English foundation; reading strategies tested across all BECE comprehension questions

First.topic: Parts of Speech (1): Nouns, Pronouns and Verbs
subtopics: Nouns: meaning and types (common, proper, collective, abstract)
Pronouns: meaning, types (personal, possessive, reflexive) and functions
Verbs: meaning, types (transitive, intransitive, auxiliary, lexical, regular, irregular)
learning_objectives: Identify, classify and use nouns, pronouns and verbs correctly in sentences
Distinguish transitive from intransitive verbs
Use the correct pronoun to replace a noun
First.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Parts of Speech; Section C of BECE Paper 1

First.topic: Parts of Speech (2): Adjectives, Adverbs, Prepositions and Conjunctions
subtopics: Adjectives: definition, types and comparison (positive, comparative, superlative; absolute adjectives)
Adverbs: definition, types and functions
Prepositions: definition, types and functions in sentences
Conjunctions: definition, types (subordinating and coordinating) and functions
Introduction to adverbials: features, functions and types
learning_objectives: Identify adjectives, adverbs, prepositions and conjunctions in context
Use comparison of adjectives correctly
Construct sentences demonstrating the correct use of each part of speech
First.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Parts of Speech; Section C Paper 1; foundation for WAEC/NECO grammar

First.topic: Reading Comprehension: Main and Supporting Ideas
subtopics: Identifying main ideas and supporting ideas in a passage
Differentiating between main and supporting ideas
Answering comprehension questions at knowledge, translation and interpretation levels
Vocabulary development in context (family, school settings)
learning_objectives: Distinguish main ideas from supporting details in a passage
Answer comprehension questions at different cognitive levels
Infer the meaning of unfamiliar words from context
First.exam_relevance: BECE Reading — Section A of Paper 1; core comprehension skill tested in all BECE papers

First.topic: Composition Writing: Introduction
subtopics: Meaning and types of composition (Narrative, Descriptive, Argumentative, Expository)
Elements of a good composition: introduction, body, conclusion
Paragraph writing: arranging ideas in logical sequence
Narrative essay: recounting a personal experience
Descriptive essay: describing a place (e.g. a Nigerian market)
learning_objectives: Identify and distinguish the four types of composition
Write a structured paragraph with a clear topic sentence
Produce a short narrative or descriptive essay
First.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3 (Essay); composition is 30 marks of BECE — high-priority topic

First.topic: Introduction to Literature in English
subtopics: What is literature? Features and types of literature (oral, historical, written)
Introduction to prose: elements of prose (plot, style, characterisation, setting, theme)
Introduction to drama: types and elements of drama
Introduction to folktales: definition, types and features (didactic, entertaining, magical)
Figures of speech: simile, metaphor, irony
learning_objectives: Define literature and identify its major genres
Describe the key elements of prose and drama
Identify and use basic figures of speech (simile and metaphor)
First.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2 (Literature); covers drama, prose, poetry and oral literature

Second.topic: Oral English: Vowel Sounds (Continued) and Tenses
subtopics: Pure vowel sounds: /u/, /u:/, /ʌ/, /3:/, /ə/ (schwa)
Contrasting vowel sounds: /ɒ/ and /ɜ:/, /a:/ and /æ/
Tenses: Simple Present, Simple Past, Present Continuous, Past Continuous
Future tense using 'will' and 'shall'
Making correct sentences using all tenses
learning_objectives: Produce and contrast a range of pure English vowel sounds
Form and use the simple present, simple past, continuous and future tenses correctly
Write sentences demonstrating accurate tense usage
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Tenses; Section C Paper 1; Spoken English — Section D Paper 1

Second.topic: Formal and Informal Letter Writing
subtopics: Revision of informal letter features (one address, salutation, body, closing)
Formal letter features: two addresses, opening salutation, body, closing, signature
Writing an informal letter: to a cousin about your new school
Writing a formal letter of permission to be absent from school
Guided formal letter: a complaint letter about bad roads
learning_objectives: Identify and write the features of formal and informal letters correctly
Produce a complete, correctly formatted formal letter
Adapt tone and register for formal and informal audiences
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3; letter writing is a fixed option in every BECE and WAEC Paper 3

Second.topic: Reading Comprehension: Critical and Projective Reading
subtopics: Reading to understand the author's mood and tone (anger, joy, doubt, disappointment)
Reading to make projections and draw conclusions
Reading to interpret diagrams, maps and sketches
Vocabulary development: child labour, health, environment
learning_objectives: Identify the author's mood and tone from language clues
Make inferences and projections based on a passage
Interpret visual information integrated with text
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Reading — author's mood and critical evaluation; Section A Paper 1

Second.topic: Composition: Expository and Argumentative Essays
subtopics: Expository essay: structure and purpose (e.g. HIV/AIDS, the Ebola virus)
Argumentative essay: presenting a case for or against (e.g. transport safety)
Arranging ideas in logical sequence
Guided composition: the Federal Roads Safety Commission
Festival composition: a festival in my village
learning_objectives: Distinguish expository from argumentative essays
Write a complete, well-structured expository or argumentative essay
Use appropriate linking words and logical sequencing
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3; expository and argumentative are always listed as essay options

Second.topic: Literature: Poetry, Myths and Legends
subtopics: Introduction to poetry: definition and features (concise, unique, high language)
Types of poetry: epic, lyric, dramatic
Oral and written poetry; differences
Myths and Legends: features (magic, supernatural, superstition)
Comparing African and non-African myths and legends; moral lessons
learning_objectives: Define poetry and identify its major types and features
Compare oral and written poetry
Identify key features of myths and legends and extract moral lessons
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Poetry and Oral Literature; Paper 2 Section on poetry

Third.topic: Oral English: Diphthongs and Consonant Sounds
subtopics: Diphthongs: /ei/, /oʊ/, /aʊ/, /iə/, /eə/, /ɔi/, /ai/, /ʊə/
Identification of diphthongs in words
Introduction to consonant sounds; consonant clusters (asks, school, thrive, respect)
Word boundaries and linking sounds
Contrasting diphthongs: /ei/ and /iə/, /ʊə/ and /əʊ/
learning_objectives: Identify and produce the eight English diphthongs
Pronounce consonant clusters and word-boundary linking sounds correctly
Distinguish between minimal pairs using diphthongs
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — Section D Paper 1; diphthong recognition is tested in multiple-choice

Third.topic: Grammar: Active and Passive Voice, and Tag Questions
subtopics: Identifying active and passive verbs in passages
Transforming active sentences to passive and vice versa
Polar questions vs. tag questions
Forming correct tag questions in the present, past and future tenses
Revision of adverbials (frequency, manner, place)
learning_objectives: Transform sentences between active and passive voice
Form and use question tags correctly
Identify and use adverbials in sentences
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Active/Passive voice and Question tags; Section C Paper 1

subtopics: Identifying topic sentences in paragraphs
Identifying key ideas and distinguishing from supporting details
Summary writing: using your own words; techniques and tips
Reading for evaluation: analysing facts, opinions and deductions
Vocabulary: spelling drills and context clues
learning_objectives: Identify topic sentences and key ideas in paragraphs
Write a concise summary in your own words
Evaluate a passage for facts, opinions and logical deductions
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Summary writing in Paper 3; summary is a compulsory question in WAEC Paper 3

subtopics: Revision of all essay types: Narrative, Descriptive, Argumentative, Expository
Oral composition: presenting ideas clearly (school rules topic)
Differentiating argumentative from expository essays
Revision of letter writing (formal and informal)
Proofreading and editing written work
learning_objectives: Identify the distinguishing features of all four essay types
Produce polished written and oral compositions
Proofread and self-edit written work
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Paper 3 — final revision of all composition types before examination

Third.topic: Literature Revision: Prose, Drama, Poetry and Figures of Speech
subtopics: Prose: types (narrative, descriptive), features (plot, characterisation, style, setting, theme)
Drama: dramatisation of selected scenes from recommended text
Poetry: figures of speech — simile, metaphor, euphemism, pun
Turning similes into metaphors and vice versa
Revision of the recommended prose and drama texts
learning_objectives: Analyse prose and drama using literary terms
Identify and explain advanced figures of speech
Demonstrate understanding of recommended texts through questions
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2; figures of speech, prose and drama analysis are always tested

First.topic: Oral English: Revision of Vowel and Consonant Sounds
subtopics: Revision of pure vowel sounds
Consonant sounds with emphasis on consonant clusters
The /iə/ sound: ear, clear, hear, bear
The /əʊ/ sound: go, blow, mow, know
Contrasting consonants: /p/ and /t/, /f/ and /v/, /v/ and /r/
learning_objectives: Produce and distinguish all learned vowel and consonant sounds
Contrast minimal pairs involving consonant and vowel sounds
First.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — Section D; sound contrasts tested in Paper 1 multiple-choice

First.topic: Grammar: Voice, Tenses and Direct/Indirect Speech
subtopics: Revision of transitive and intransitive verbs
Active voice and passive voice (extension from JSS1)
Tenses: Present Continuous, Past Perfect
Direct speech: statements, commands and requests
Indirect speech: rules for changing tense, pronoun, time reference
learning_objectives: Identify transitive and intransitive verbs and use them correctly
Transform sentences between active and passive voice
Convert between direct and indirect speech accurately
First.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Direct/Indirect speech and tenses; Section C Paper 1

First.topic: Reading Comprehension: Writer's Purpose and Critical Reading
subtopics: Reading to understand the writer's purpose (to inform, persuade, entertain, etc.)
Critical reading: meaning, steps and process
Identifying facts and opinions in a passage
Vocabulary development: word families (hospital, police, places of worship)
learning_objectives: Identify and explain the writer's purpose in a passage
Distinguish facts from opinions in written texts
Build vocabulary through word family study
First.exam_relevance: BECE Reading — writer's purpose; Section A Paper 1

First.topic: Composition Writing: Descriptive and Narrative Essays
subtopics: Elements of composition: pre-writing, writing, editing
Descriptive essay: a place of interest, a local festival, my favourite teacher
Narrative essay: what I do every day; my last holiday
Letter writing: a letter to a father about a new school; a letter of invitation
Report writing: introduction, outline and features
learning_objectives: Follow the writing process (pre-writing, drafting, editing) for any essay type
Write a well-structured descriptive or narrative essay
Write a formal letter of invitation
First.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3; all listed essay types appear as BECE options

First.topic: Literature: Prose Features, Folktales and Figures of Speech
subtopics: Prose features (revision): setting, theme, characterisation, plot
Features of folktales: didactic, entertaining, riddles, magical elements
Differentiating a novella from a novel
Figures of speech: alliteration, onomatopoeia, personification
Use of recommended prose text
learning_objectives: Analyse prose using all major literary elements
Identify features of folktales in a given text
Define and identify alliteration, onomatopoeia and personification
First.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2; prose and oral literature analysis

Second.topic: Oral English: Intonation, Stress and Rhythm
subtopics: Intonation: identification of patterns in statements, questions and commands
Stress: correct points of pauses in words (im'port vs. 'import; 're'fuse vs. re'fuse)
Rhythm: identification of appropriate rhythm in a given poem
Consonant sounds: /s/ and /z/, /k/ and /g/, /m/, /n/, /ŋ/
learning_objectives: Identify and use rising and falling intonation correctly
Apply correct word stress to distinguish meaning
Demonstrate correct rhythm in a poem
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — intonation, stress and rhythm; Section D Paper 1

Second.topic: Grammar: Reported Speech (Commands and Requests) and Punctuation
subtopics: Reported commands: transformation rules
Reported requests using 'asked' and modal verbs
Adverbials: present tense adverbials; frequency adverbials
Punctuation marks: full stop, comma, question mark, colon, semicolon
learning_objectives: Convert direct commands and requests into reported speech
Use reported speech rules accurately for all sentence types
Apply punctuation marks correctly in written work
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — Reported speech and punctuation; Section C Paper 1

Second.topic: Reading Comprehension: Speed Reading and Summarising
subtopics: Speed reading techniques: surveying, scanning, skimming
Avoiding vocalisation and regressive reading
Identifying main and supporting ideas (revision)
Reading to summarise a selected passage
Dialogue: reading and interpreting dialogue passages
learning_objectives: Apply scanning, skimming and speed-reading techniques
Summarise a passage extracting only the key information
Interpret information from a written dialogue
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Reading and Summary Writing — Section A and Paper 3

Second.topic: Composition: Reports, Argumentative and Narrative Essays
subtopics: Report writing: a witnessed accident; structure and language
Argumentative essay: herbal vs orthodox medicines
Narrative essay: my last birthday
Expository essay: entrepreneurship in Nigeria
Story writing: the meeting
learning_objectives: Write a structured accident report with accurate factual language
Construct a convincing argument with supporting evidence
Produce a polished narrative essay
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3; report writing is a distinct BECE option; argumentative essays always appear

Second.topic: Literature: Drama, Figures of Speech and Recommended Texts
subtopics: Drama features: theme, stage, action, plot, characters, props, costumes
Themes and characterisation in the recommended drama text
Figures of speech: irony, euphemism, hyperbole — definition, identification, use
Short play writing: the reward of truancy
Summary of recommended prose and drama texts
learning_objectives: Analyse a drama text using all major literary elements
Define and use irony, euphemism and hyperbole correctly
Write a short play demonstrating understanding of dramatic structure
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2; drama analysis, figures of speech and text-based questions

Third.topic: Oral English: Consonant Contrasts and Review
subtopics: Consonant sounds: /t/ and /d/, /p/ and /b/, /tʃ/ and /ʃ/, /θ/ and /ð/
Contrast between vowels /u/ and /u:/, /ɜ:/ and /ʌ/
Review of all consonant and vowel sounds
Word and sentence stress revision
learning_objectives: Distinguish and produce contrasting consonant pairs correctly
Demonstrate accuracy in all vowel and consonant sounds covered in JSS2
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — final oral revision; Section D Paper 1

Third.topic: Grammar: Conjunctions, Synonyms, Antonyms and Causative Verbs
subtopics: Conjunctions: 'although' and 'whereas' as contrasts; 'despite' and 'in spite of'
Synonyms: finding replacement words that fit the context
Antonyms: opposite meanings
Causative verbs: e.g. 'blacken — to cause to become black'
Adverbs of place: by the gate, in the school, on the field
learning_objectives: Use contrast conjunctions correctly in complex sentences
Identify synonyms and antonyms in the context of comprehension passages
Construct sentences using causative verbs
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy and Vocabulary — conjunctions, synonyms tested in Section B and C Paper 1

subtopics: Review of formal and informal letters and all essay types
Dialogue writing: conventions and format
Compositions on social themes: The Rich also Cry; Pride goes before a fall; The day I would not like to remember
Writing a composition on government responsibilities
learning_objectives: Write all studied letter and essay types without errors
Produce a correctly formatted written dialogue
Write thematic compositions on social issues relevant to Nigeria
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Paper 3 — final composition revision; social themes are common essay prompts

Third.topic: Literature Revision: Myths, Drama and Recommended Texts
subtopics: Myths and legends: themes (magic, supernatural, superstition), moral import
Drama: characterisation, diction, plot and theme in recommended text
Prose text review: setting, plot, characterisation, moral lessons
Use of literary terms in examination answers
Review of all figures of speech studied in JSS2
learning_objectives: Analyse myths and legends using key literary features
Answer examination questions on recommended prose and drama texts
Use literary terms accurately in written responses
Third.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2; all text-based and oral literature questions

First.topic: Grammar Revision and Modal Forms
subtopics: Revision of all parts of speech (nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions, conjunctions)
Modal verbs: will, can, could, may, might — direct and indirect forms
Expressing emotions using verb + preposition constructions
Adverbs of frequency: often, always, occasionally
Changing positive statements to negatives using 'not'; idiomatic expressions
learning_objectives: Demonstrate accurate use of all parts of speech
Use modal verbs correctly in direct and indirect forms
Construct sentences using adverbs of frequency and idiomatic expressions
First.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — all grammar structures; Section C of Paper 1

First.topic: Oral English: Schwa, Stress, Intonation and Consonant Contrasts
subtopics: The schwa /ə/ sound: about, doctor, above, teacher, pastor
Stress and intonation continued (revision and extension)
Consonant contrasts: /ʒ/ and /dʒ/ — leisure/ledger; /d/, /ð/, /θ/, /z/
Consonant contrasts: /s/, /ʃ/, /tʃ/ — ceiling, short, machine, cheque
Consonants /w/ and /j/ — wonder/yellow
learning_objectives: Produce the schwa sound in unstressed syllables
Distinguish and produce contrasting consonant sounds accurately
Apply stress and intonation knowledge to spoken English exercises
First.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — Section D Paper 1; final JSS-level oral English preparation

First.topic: Reading Comprehension: Scanning, Skimming and Deduction
subtopics: Scanning for main points
Skimming for specific information
Practising all reading rates: scanning, skimming and normal
Reading to differentiate facts from opinions
Reading to make deductions from a passage
Identifying topic sentences in passages
learning_objectives: Apply scanning and skimming strategies fluently
Distinguish facts from opinions and make logical deductions
Identify topic sentences to aid comprehension and summary
First.exam_relevance: BECE Reading — Section A Paper 1; all BECE comprehension techniques tested here

First.topic: Composition: Informal Letters, Narratives, Debates and Descriptive Essays
subtopics: Informal letter: a letter to my best friend about my academic plans
Writing to a pen-pal: features and tone
Narrative story: 'All that glitters is not gold'
Descriptive essay: My favourite subject
Debate writing: Corruption is worse than armed robbery; Child trafficking is worse than stealing
Care-for-a-vehicle instructions essay
learning_objectives: Write a pen-pal letter with appropriate tone and format
Construct a well-structured debate essay presenting two sides
Produce a polished narrative using figurative language
First.exam_relevance: BECE Writing — Paper 3; debate/argumentative and narrative essays are common BECE options

First.topic: Literature: Fiction, Poetry and Rhyme Schemes
subtopics: Fiction vs. non-fiction: definitions and examples
Poetry analysis: tone, mood, theme, language
Types of rhyme scheme: ABAB, AABB, ABCABC
Identification of literary devices in a selected poem
Introduction to rhyme scheme (ABAB, cross rhyme, couplet)
Comprehensive study of recommended prose text: characterisation, theme, plot
learning_objectives: Distinguish fiction from non-fiction with examples
Analyse a poem for theme, mood, tone and rhyme scheme
Identify and name literary devices in poetry
First.exam_relevance: BECE Literature — Paper 2; poetry analysis and rhyme scheme are regularly tested

Second.topic: Oral English: Consonant Contrasts and Nasal Sounds
subtopics: Consonant contrasts: /t/ and /θ/ — tin/thin, tick/thick
Consonant contrasts: /t/ and /s/ — tailor/summer
Nasal sounds: /m/, /n/ and /ŋ/ — mentor, knotty, fling
Contrasting vowels: /ʌ/ and /a:/ — much/march; /æ/ and /3:/ — bath/birth
Vowels: /ɜ:/ and /ɔ/ — brought/hot; /u/ and /u:/ — pull/pool
learning_objectives: Produce and contrast nasal sounds and consonant pairs
Demonstrate mastery of vowel contrasts
Apply phonetic knowledge to BECE-style oral English questions
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Spoken English — Section D Paper 1; final oral English preparation before BECE

Second.topic: Grammar: Sentence Types and Pronouns
subtopics: The simple sentence: subject, verb, object/complement
The compound sentence: joining with coordinating conjunctions
Prepositions expressing relations with people: with, for, against
Reflexive pronouns: myself, himself, themselves
Relative pronouns: who, whom, which, that
Personal pronouns: rules guiding correct use
Demonstrative pronouns vs. demonstrative adjectives
learning_objectives: Construct simple and compound sentences correctly
Use all types of pronouns accurately
Distinguish demonstrative pronouns from demonstrative adjectives
Second.exam_relevance: BECE Grammatical Accuracy — pronoun use, sentence types; Section C Paper 1

Second.topic: Reading Comprehension: Critical Evaluation and Listening
subtopics: Reading for critical evaluation: detecting facts, opinions, logicality, bias
Listening for implied meanings in speeches and passages
Listening to get main gist from paragraphs
Listening to interpret ideas
Reading to summarise paragraphs
Vocabulary development: tourism, conflict resolution
learning_objectives: Critically evaluate a passage for facts, opinions and bias
Extract main gist and implied meanings from spoken and written texts
Summarise paragraphs accurately

Second.topic: Composition: Article Writing, Expository and Argumentative Essays
subtopics: Article writing: the evils of youth unemployment; features and structure
Expository essay: drug addiction; population explosion
Argumentative essay: Teachers are better than farmers in society
Informal letters: to a friend; to a grandparent
The School Prize-Giving Day: report or descriptive composition

Second.topic: Literature: Poetry, Drama and Prose Revision
subtopics: Literary devices in a selected poem: simile, metaphor, alliteration, irony, hyperbole
Drama: lead students to write a playlet
Drama text: theme, setting, characterisation
Prose: identification of prose features; summarising the prose text
Review of folktales; short stories — the tortoise and the pattern on his shell

Write a short playlet demonstrating understanding of dramatic structure

subtopics: Review of all parts of speech: nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions, prepositions, interjections
Review of tenses, active/passive voice, direct/indirect speech, question tags
Review of all vowel sounds (monotones) and consonant sounds

BECE past questions: grammatical accuracy and spoken English sections
learning_objectives: Demonstrate mastery of all JSS1–JSS3 grammar topics
Answer BECE-format grammatical accuracy and spoken English questions confidently

subtopics: Revision of Narrative, Descriptive, Argumentative and Expository essays
Revision of formal and informal letters
Summary writing: rules, techniques, BECE-format practice
BECE past Paper 3 questions: essay and summary
Proofreading for grammar, spelling, punctuation and expression

subtopics: Review of prose: types, features, recommended prose texts

Review of drama: types, features, costumes, props, performance
Review of oral literature: folktales, myths, legends

First.topic: Oral English: Sounds, Stress and Intonation
subtopics: Review of all 20 vowel sounds and 24 consonant sounds from JSS
Word and sentence stress patterns

Spoken English for communication: presentations and formal speech
WAEC/NECO Spoken English format and expectations

First.topic: Grammar: Complex Sentences and Conditional Clauses

subtopics: Reading for main and supporting ideas (advanced passage level)
Reading for tone, mood and author's attitude in SS-level passages
Critical evaluation of arguments and evidence in a passage

Registers: language of law, medicine, commerce, education

First.topic: Composition (1): Narrative and Descriptive Essays
subtopics: Narrative essay: advanced techniques (flashback, suspense, dialogue)

Personal essay: writing about experiences and observations
The writing process: planning, drafting, revising and publishing

First.topic: Literature: Introduction to Prose and the Novel
subtopics: Prose as a literary genre: types (fiction, non-fiction, short story, novel, novella)

Studying an African prose text: themes, setting, social context

subtopics: Reported speech: rules for tense, pronoun and time reference changes
Reporting questions, commands and requests
Modal verbs: will, would, shall, should, can, could, may, might, must, ought to
Sentence patterns (SV, SVO, SVOC, SVOA)
Clause functions: subject, object, complement, adverbial
